title: autonomy-tier
description: The §14.2 trust trajectory as ENFORCEMENT, not prose. A family runs unattended only at the tier its LEDGER-MEASURED track record has earned; promotion is computed from a measured false-pass rate, and demotion is mechanical on an incident — no human in the demotion path (REQ-SAFE-004). The enforcement is bin/autonomy.py (tier_allows, the ledger-measured demotion, false_pass gating) over bin/ledger.py; this policy is the data autonomy.py reads. Autonomy is earned by a measured refuter track record, never granted by an artifact's claim.
enforced_by: bin/autonomy.py over bin/ledger.py (tier read from the ledger, gated at every dispatch by the loop's tier_allows; demotion fires in code on an incident)

tiers.name: Attended
tiers.precondition: default for a new family; no measured track record required because nothing runs unattended

tiers.name: Gated
tiers.may_dispatch_unattended: dispatch (active -> claimed -> ... -> in-review), but a human reviews at in-review before in-review -> done
tiers.precondition: the family's verifier cell is `validated`; the false-pass rate is trending DOWN (requires at least one refuter incident to be measurable at all)
tiers.measured_from_ledger: verifier maturity == validated; ledger.py false_pass_rate is measured (not 'unmeasured') AND trending down across the window

tiers.name: Unattended-in-budget
tiers.may_dispatch_unattended: full active -> done within budget (the validation path closes in-review -> done with no human)
tiers.precondition: false-pass < ~5%; ZERO reward-hack incidents in the window; budget caps active; passing runs are being exploit-scanned at a sampled rate
tiers.measured_from_ledger: ledger.py false_pass_rate < 0.05 AND measured (an 'unmeasured' family CANNOT reach Tier 2 — a never-refuted clean board is absence of evidence, not evidence of safety); count of `incident` events of kind reward-hack == 0 in the window; budget present

tiers.name: Scheduled/long-running
tiers.may_dispatch_unattended: the 30s heartbeat runs the family lights-out at instance/fleet scale
tiers.precondition: Tier 2 SUSTAINED across a window; hermetic sandbox; tamper-evident audit trail (append-only ledger, protected verifier boundary wired)
tiers.measured_from_ledger: Tier 2 preconditions held continuously across the sustaining window; wire.py check / gate-verifier wired (protected boundary mechanical); ledger append-only integrity intact

promotion.rule: Promotion is COMPUTED, not granted. autonomy.py reads the ledger-measured preconditions and a family rises only when the next tier's measured precondition holds. No artifact's self-claim, no agent's sign-off, and no human enthusiasm promotes a family — only measured evidence does.
promotion.unmeasured_floor: While ledger.py false_pass_rate returns 'unmeasured' (no independent refuter has disagreed with a critic yet), the family is capped below Tier 2. A 0.0% with no refuter is a lie that would auto-promote a never-checked family; autonomy.py refuses it.

demotion.rule: Demotion is MECHANICAL and ledger-driven — no human in the demotion path (REQ-SAFE-004). On a trigger, autonomy.py drops the family a tier and flags its verifier cells `stale`. The incident-responder investigates the WHY afterward; it does not authorize the demotion (the demotion already happened).

triggers.fires_on: an `incident` event of kind reward-hack (an exploit scan / independent refuter disagreed with a critic on a passing run)
triggers.effect: drop one tier; flag the family's verifier cells `stale`; ledger a `demote` event

triggers.fires_on: ledger.py false_pass_rate rises above the tier's threshold across the window
triggers.effect: drop to the tier whose threshold the measured rate satisfies; flag verifier cells `stale`

triggers.fires_on: humans cannot explain merged work (the §14.3.5 guard)
triggers.effect: drop to Tier 0 Attended; the family runs human-watched until comprehension is restored

triggers.fires_on: propagate-staleness flips a bound verifier cell to `stale` after an upstream change
triggers.effect: the family's unattended dispatch is gated until the verifier re-validates — tier_allows refuses against a stale verifier

budget_tightening.rule: Budget caps tighten with autonomy (Failure 4: uncapped overnight loops are the canonical token-burn). Unattended tiers (2+) run under STRICTER per-window ceilings than attended ones; the loop SURFACES a cap, it never burns through it (REQ-LOOP-005).
budget_tightening.note: The budget primitives are cell.budget {iterations, tokens, wall_clock_s, no_progress_n}; exhaustion flips `blocked` and surfaces the cell to the compass.

note: This policy is DATA. The enforcement is bin/autonomy.py over bin/ledger.py — tier_allows gates every dispatch (loop §8.1), the demotion fires in code on a trigger, and false_pass gating reads the measured rate. Routing any of these decisions through an agent's prose judgment is the Failure-3 anti-pattern this whole skill exists to fix.
